comment: Colour is not used as the only visual means of conveying information or indicating an action.
All user interface elements, as well as their states (focus, hover, select, check, visited/unvisited, expand/collapse and outline) have a colour contrast of 3:1.
Links that open in a new tab/window should alert the user.
Text is still visible and readable when resized to at least 200%.
Icons are accessible using a keyboard only.
The purpose of each link can be determined from the text alone.
Headings and labels describe topic or purpose.
Focus is visible when navigating using a keyboard
Selecting a link does not change the context of the content without notifying the user.
Where links are used as part of the navigation this is consistent across the website.
Text is visible/readable when the line spacing is at least 1.5 times the font size, letter spacing is at least 0.12 times the font size, and word spacing is at least 0.16 times the font size.
